blocks.content: Start monitoring a host or service by installing an integration. An integration sets up the Grafana Alloy collector and prebuilt dashboards for a host, database, or web server, so you can see data in minutes.
You'll need a host or service to monitor, terminal and admin access to it, and outbound HTTPS on port 443 to `*.grafana.net` allowed.
You start on the **Add new connection** page, filtered to integrations. Choose the one that matches your host or service, then run its install command.
Search for the integration that matches what you want to monitor, for example a Linux, macOS, or Windows host, a database, or a web server, then click its tile.
On the **Configuration details** tab, copy the install command and run it in your terminal. The command includes a token, so treat it like a secret and don't share it.
Optionally set a **Hostname value** to label this host. Use the **Advanced integration** toggle to send only the metrics the integration needs, which helps control cost.
You've installed an integration. Alloy is now collecting metrics and sending them to Grafana Cloud.
See your metrics on the dashboard the integration installed.
Open the main menu and click **Dashboards**.
Open the folder the integration created, then open one of its prebuilt dashboards.
The dashboard stays empty until the first scrape, then data appears. You can adjust the scrape interval later to balance detail and cost.
You're viewing your first metrics in Grafana Cloud.

Metrics usage scales with what you send. The **Advanced integration** option and the scrape interval are your main cost levers. Refer to [Manage metrics costs](/docs/grafana-cloud/cost-management-and-billing/reduce-costs/metrics-costs/).
**Return to Get started with Grafana Cloud** to pick another path.
